{"id":"project-LICHEN-ydpn","title":"announce: seq_num increment callback exception silently swallowed","description":"File: python/src/lichen/announce/scheduler.py:144-148

The _increment_seq method catches and logs exceptions from the persistence callback but continues:

```python
if self._on_seq_change:
    try:
        self._on_seq_change(self._seq_num)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning('seq_change callback failed: %s', e)
```

If the persistence callback fails (e.g., disk full, I/O error), the seq_num has already been incremented in memory but NOT persisted. On node restart, the seq_num could be lower than what was transmitted, causing peers to reject announces as stale (STALE_SEQNUM).

The failure is logged at WARNING level, which may be missed in production. Should either:
1. Retry persistence or raise to prevent announce transmission
2. Log at ERROR level with more context about the consequences
3. Implement a 'dirty' flag that prevents announces until persistence succeeds","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:18:35.034546Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:18:35.03458Z"}
